title: The Relationship Between Sample Statistics, Sampling Distribution, and Standard Error
description: A visual explanation of how standard error measures the variability of a sample statistic (like the mean) across multiple samples from the same population.
elements: Population (with true mean μ and standard deviation σ)
Multiple random samples (size n) drawn from the population
Sample mean (x̄) calculated for each sample
Distribution of all sample means (Sampling Distribution)
relationships: The population is the source from which many samples are drawn
Each sample yields a single estimate (x̄) for the population mean
The collection of all sample means forms a new distribution—the sampling distribution
The standard deviation of this sampling distribution is called the Standard Error, which quantifies the precision of the sample mean as an estimate of the population mean
